# slidekit Documentation

A robots.txt-style index of the slidekit documentation for LLMs. slidekit is an
HTTP service that turns Markdown into **one fully self-contained Slidev HTML
deck** (CSS, JS and fonts inlined; presenter mode; works offline). Each entry
below has a title, path, and one-line summary. For the complete documentation
inlined into a single file, see [docs/llms-full.txt](docs/llms-full.txt).

> Default local base URL: http://localhost:4030 — OpenAPI 3.1 spec at `/openapi.json`.
> To render: `POST /render` with the Markdown as the
> body (`content-type: text/markdown`) or a `multipart/form-data` file field; when
> `SLIDEKIT_API_KEYS` is set, send `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

## Start here

- [README](README.md): What slidekit is, quickstart (serve / curl / Docker / single binary), the endpoint + `/render` query-param tables, and links to everything else.
- [Example deck](examples/demo.md): A generic Slidev deck exercising cover, two-cols, `v-clicks`, Mermaid and a code fence with the bundled themes.
- [Examples README](examples/README.md): Sample input decks — inputs to the service, never bundled — with preview and render commands.

## API & usage

- Endpoints: `GET /` (JSON service descriptor), `POST /render` (Markdown → one self-contained HTML deck), `GET /themes`, `GET /health` (liveness), `GET /ready` (readiness; 503 while draining), `GET /metrics` (Prometheus), `GET /v1/stats/builds` (authenticated hourly product statistics), `GET /jobs/{id}` (async job status), `GET /jobs/{id}/result` (async job result deck), `GET /openapi.json`, `GET /llms.txt`, `GET /llms-full.txt`.
- `POST /render` query params (all optional): `theme` (CSS overlay; see `/themes`), `title`, `author`, `tags`, `description`, `image`, `imageAlt`, `url` (canonical), `siteName`, `locale`, `lang`, `robots`, `themeColor`, `twitterSite`, `twitterCreator`, `favicon` (emoji/data: URI, inlined), `download=1`, `async=1` (→ 202 + `/jobs/{id}`), `callback` (async webhook; host must be in `SLIDEKIT_WEBHOOK_ALLOW`). Body: raw `text/markdown`, or `multipart/form-data` file field (`file`/`markdown`/`md`). Responses carry a strong `ETag` (→ `304` on `If-None-Match`); identical renders are cached; responses are gzip-compressed on `Accept-Encoding`.
- OpenAPI 3.1 is generated from a route registry, with the `theme` enum and auth scheme injected dynamically — fetch the live spec at `/openapi.json`.

## Theming & icons

- [Theming & Icons](docs/THEMING-AND-ICONS.md): The two-layer model (a fixed Slidev base theme + a per-request CSS overlay selected via `?theme=`), design tokens, light/dark, writing a custom theme, and bundled icons (Material Symbols, Carbon, Phosphor). Note: a deck's `theme:` frontmatter is normalized to the base theme, so choose the look via `?theme=`.

## Operating & deployment

- [Configuration](docs/CONFIGURATION.md): Every environment variable (binding, auth, rate limit, build caps, themes) with defaults — 12-factor, optional `.env`.
- [Deployment](docs/DEPLOYMENT.md): Run as a Node service, single self-contained binary, or Docker; production checklist, `/health` + `/ready` probes, graceful shutdown, structured stdout logging.
- [Security](SECURITY.md): Trust model — `/render` runs a real Slidev/Vite build, so uploading Markdown is equivalent to code execution; deploy behind auth and OS isolation. Built-in mitigations and their limits.
- [.env.example](.env.example): Annotated template of every configuration variable.

## Development

- [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): Request lifecycle, the `src/` module map, OpenAPI-from-route-registry, and how the self-contained binary is packaged.
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): Dev setup, guidelines (server code uses only Node built-ins, tests, the self-contained-output guarantee), and the release process.
- [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Contributor Covenant v2.1.
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): Notable changes per release (Keep a Changelog / SemVer).
